tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: With only ten percent left and no competing deadlines, I can finish this assignment quickly without sacrificing my sleep discipline or other long-term goals.
I misjudged the timeline, leaving this unfinished despite my belief that I could finish quickly. The missed deadline is a direct conflict with my academic value priority.

desires: Finish this assignment immediately to restore my academic integrity and stop the internal friction of having failed a self-imposed deadline.
Avoid looking incompetent or unreliable to Professor Assignment by submitting before they notice the delay, despite my low desire for social closeness with them.
Protect my remaining energy reserves so I don't collapse tomorrow and jeopardize my long-term planning horizon.

goals.description: Submit the assignment immediately to secure the grade and prevent the catastrophic loss of academic standing caused by missing the 13:30 deadline.
goals.rationale: My value priority for academics (0.5) is dominant; avoiding interpersonal friction with Professor Assignment requires a flawless, on-time submission. The deadline just passed, so the cost of failure is now immediate and irreversible. I must prioritize this over conserving energy because the anxiety of an incomplete record outweighs my current fatigue.

goals.description: Execute a strict 'shutdown protocol' immediately after submission to recover physical stamina and sleep debt before tomorrow's coursework block.
goals.rationale: While health and long-term planning are important, they cannot override the immediate academic crisis. However, once the task is done, protecting my remaining energy reserves becomes critical to avoid collapsing tomorrow and jeopardizing my long-term horizon.
